landing.seoBanner: SEO LANDING PAGE ACTIVE • META TARGET: aquaculture management for absentee owners
landing.heroTag: Absolute Oversight for Catfish Farm Owners
landing.heroDesc: The ultimate catfish feed management app built for remote fish farm monitoring in the Philippines. Protect your margins, eliminate staff feed theft or neglect, and optimize your Feed Conversion Ratio (FCR).
landing.hiddenBleedTitle: The Hidden Bleed: Why Remote Feeding Fails
landing.hiddenBleedDesc: Remote catfish farm owners in the Philippines face a serious trust-and-visibility gap. When you cannot physically step onto the pond dike every day, you are exposed to costly operational leaks.
landing.feat1Body: Feed represents up to 70% of total farm costs. Guesswork overfeeding wastes tens of thousands of pesos in bags of pellets, while unchecked staff can quietly resell feed bags off-site.
landing.feat2Body: Hired staff get busy, forget scheduled times, or skip hot afternoon feeding cycles entirely. Without an immutable digital log, missed feedings are hidden until harvest day reveals stunted weights.
landing.feat3Body: Over-dispensing feed doesn't grow catfish faster; it decays the pond bottom, triggers toxic ammonia spikes, and raises your FCR. This directly destroys your net profit margins.
landing.howItWorksDesc: No complex vision systems or cameras that fail in muddy waters. OptiFeed uses physical ground truth coupled with robust automated execution.
landing.step1Body: Your staff pulls a random net sample of fish on-site, counts them, and logs their average weight and length directly into the browser.
landing.step2Body: OptiFeed instantly recalculates the optimal feeding quantity for upcoming scheduled feedings. It shifts the feed load precisely to prevent waste.
landing.step3Body: The automated ESP32 feeder receives the updated schedule weights instantly. Feed is dispensed exactly on schedule, and you track the live logs remotely.
landing.remoteDesc: Whether you are working from a condo in Manila, traveling abroad, or managing other business interests, OptiFeed gives you a clear window into your pond operations.
landing.remote1Rest: Know in 1 second if your farm network goes offline.
landing.remote2Rest: Every biomass entry and feeding trigger has an immutable digital timestamp. No hidden adjustments.
landing.remote3Rest: Interactive sparklines display current feed efficiency so you can step in before costs escalate.
landing.trustQuote: "Developed with catfish farm owners in Iloilo and Western Visayas, aligning with the Bureau of Fisheries and Aquatic Resources (BFAR) guidelines for sustainable animal nutrition and cage-pond aquaculture development."
landing.ctaTitle: Ready to secure your catfish pond?
landing.ctaDesc: Access your remote feed management console, connect your automated ESP32 hardware, and start saving bags of feed today.
landing.footerCopyright: © 2026 OptiFeed Philippines. All rights reserved. Western Visayas Aquaculture.
landing.faq1q: How do I monitor my fish farm remotely?
landing.faq1a: OptiFeed connects directly with your pond's ESP32 automated feeder controller. As long as your feeder has an active link to the local network, you can monitor schedule times, connection state, and exactly how many grams of feed were dispensed from any phone, tablet, or desktop in real-time.
landing.faq2q: What is Feed Conversion Ratio (FCR) and why does it matter?
landing.faq2a: FCR is the measure of how efficiently your African Catfish are turning feed into body weight (Biomass). By measuring a physical sample of fish and logging it in the app, OptiFeed calculates your precise FCR trend. Lowering FCR from 1.5 to 1.1 can save up to 25% in overall feed costs.
landing.faq3q: Can I check on my farm without visiting?
landing.faq3a: Yes! OptiFeed was designed specifically for remote aquaculture management for absentee owners. You can verify that your staff completed feeding runs, view live device connectivity status, and audit the exact volumes dispensed on a reverse-chronological timeline.
landing.faq4q: Does this work with hired farm hands?
landing.faq4a: Absolutely. Instead of waiting for staff to guess, OptiFeed becomes the single source of truth. Your worker just takes a standard biomass sample and logs it. The app then sets the exact feed volume for the ESP32 automatic dispenser, eliminating manual feeding errors.
landing.faq5q: What hardware do I need for this system?
landing.faq5a: You need an ESP32 microcontroller with a relay module attached to your feeder's auger motor. OptiFeed sends the precise run-time signals directly to your controller over WiFi or cellular.

chat.title: OptiFeed Assistant
chat.intro: Hi! I'm your OptiFeed assistant. Ask me about your next feeding time, current FCR, missed feedings, or how to log a sample!
chat.error: Oops, something went wrong. Please try again.
chat.intentScheduleNoPond: I couldn't find your pond schedule. Please ensure your devices are configured.
chat.intentSchedulePaused: Your automated schedule is currently **Paused**. No feedings will occur until you resume it on the Schedule page.
chat.intentScheduleActive: Your device is active and scheduled to feed **{feedsPerDay} times per day** between **{startTime}** and **{endTime}**.
chat.intentLogSample:
To log a new biomass sample:
1. Navigate to the **Log Sample** tab in the sidebar.
2. Enter the average weight and count of your sampled fish.
3. The system will automatically calculate your new recommended daily feed volume based on your feeding rate!
chat.intentFcrNoData: I couldn't find any recent FCR reports for your pond.
chat.intentFcrActive: Your most recent Feed Conversion Ratio (FCR) is **{fcr}**. You can view your full historical performance on the Growth tab.
chat.intentMissedNoData: Good news! I don't see any recent missed feedings in your logs.
chat.intentMissedActive: I see a missed feeding event from {time}. Missed feedings usually occur when the **hopper is empty** or the device **loses its Wi-Fi connection** before dispensing. Please check the physical feeder.
chat.fallback:
I'm your OptiFeed support assistant! You can ask me things like:
- "When is the next feeding time?"
- "What is my current FCR?"
- "How do I log a sample?"
- "Why was a feeding missed?"
chat.serverError: Sorry, I ran into an error trying to fetch that data. Please try again.
